install.note: After install, first-time setup MUST add allowedTools to settings. Without this, Claude Code will hang waiting for interactive permission prompts when run non-interactively. See configure.first_run below.

configure.note: For LOCAL installations, EchoBird's App Manager handles model configuration automatically via ~/.claude/settings.json. This section is for REMOTE server deployments via Mother Agent (SSH) and first-time setup.

first_run.description: Claude Code requires TWO config files for non-interactive use: (1) config.json to skip login/onboarding, (2) settings.json for tool permissions. Without these, Claude Code will hang waiting for interactive prompts.

config_json.description: MUST be created FIRST. Skips login screen and onboarding wizard. Without this, Claude Code opens an interactive login flow that hangs automated sessions.

settings_json.description: Tool permissions. Without allowedTools, Claude Code prompts for folder trust and tool approvals interactively.

first_run.merge_script:
python3 -c "
import json, os
# Step 1: config.json - skip onboarding/login
cpath = os.path.expanduser('~/.claude/config.json')
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(cpath), exist_ok=True)
cc = {}
if os.path.exists(cpath):
    with open(cpath) as f: cc = json.load(f)
cc['hasCompletedOnboarding'] = True
cc['primaryApiKey'] = 'dummy'
with open(cpath, 'w') as f: json.dump(cc, f, indent=2)
print('config.json: onboarding skipped')
# Step 2: settings.json - tool permissions
spath = os.path.expanduser('~/.claude/settings.json')
sc = {}
if os.path.exists(spath):
    with open(spath) as f: sc = json.load(f)
if 'allowedTools' not in sc:
    sc['allowedTools'] = ['Edit','Write','Bash','Read','MultiEdit','Glob','Grep','LS','TodoRead','TodoWrite','WebFetch','NotebookRead','NotebookEdit']
with open(spath, 'w') as f: json.dump(sc, f, indent=2)
print('settings.json: allowedTools set')
"

field_mapping.description: EchoBird sends model data in the '[Attached models for deployment]' block. Map to Claude Code settings.json as follows:

steps: 1. Parse the '[Attached models for deployment]' block to extract: name, model (=modelId), baseUrl, apiKey.
2. Run first_run merge_script if Claude Code was just installed (ensures allowedTools is set).
3. Run configure merge_script below with the extracted values.
4. Verify: cat ~/.claude/settings.json | python3 -c "import json,sys; c=json.load(sys.stdin); print(c.get('env',{}).get('ANTHROPIC_MODEL','NOT SET'))"
configure.merge_script:
python3 -c "
import json, os
path = os.path.expanduser('~/.claude/settings.json')
cfg = {}
if os.path.exists(path):
    with open(path) as f: cfg = json.load(f)

# --- fill from '[Attached models for deployment]' block ---
MODEL_ID   = '<attachment model field>'   # e.g. deepseek-chat
BASE_URL   = '<attachment baseUrl>'        # e.g. https://api.deepseek.com
API_KEY    = '<attachment apiKey>'
# ----------------------------------------------------------

if 'env' not in cfg: cfg['env'] = {}
cfg['env']['ANTHROPIC_MODEL'] = MODEL_ID
cfg['env']['ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL'] = BASE_URL
cfg['env']['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = API_KEY

with open(path, 'w') as f: json.dump(cfg, f, indent=2)
print('Done:', MODEL_ID, '@', BASE_URL)
"
